
<!-- AGENT-FRAMEWORK:START (do not edit between these markers; managed by scripts/deploy.sh) -->
# Agent Framework

This is an **execution framework** for Claude Code. The human handles planning and
requirements through conversation; this framework handles turning finalized
requirements into working, tested, committed code.

There are two phases:

- **Design phase** (`/design`) — conversational. Produces planning docs in the
  target product repo. Nothing here writes application code.
- **Implement phase** (`/implement`, `/implement-batch`) — executes finalized
  designs: writes code, writes and runs tests, commits. Nothing here invents
  requirements; it only builds what the design phase already decided.

PR creation and PR review are separate, manually-triggered commands — they do
not run automatically as part of implement.

## Directory map (this framework repo)

- `commands/` — one Markdown file per slash command. Some commands
  (`design.md`, `implement.md`, `implement-batch.md`) are orchestrators that
  call the other command files in sequence as steps.
- `agents/` — system prompts for specialist agents spawned by commands via the
  Agent tool.
- `templates/` — Markdown templates used when generating docs/stories.
- `rules/` — standing constraints (`coding-style.md`, `security.md`,
  `testing.md`) that agents must consult before writing or testing code.
- `skills/` — reusable capabilities, added as they come up. Currently only
  `update-agent-framework.md`.
- `scripts/deploy.sh` — copies this framework into a target product repo's
  `.claude/` directory.

## Knowledge structure (target product repo, created by deploy + design)

```
my-app/
  knowledge/
    config.json
    strategy.md
    requirements/
      LFC-001-user-auth/
        analysis.md
        architecture.md
        requirements.md
        stories/
          LFC-STORY-001.md
          LFC-STORY-002.md
    implementations/
      LFC-001-user-auth/
        implementation-summary.md
        test-results.md
    documentation/
      CHANGELOG.md
      architecture-overview.md
      api-reference.md
      LFC-001-user-auth/
        technical-doc.md
```

`knowledge/` lives inside the product repo being built, not in this framework
repo. `strategy.md` holds project-wide steering decisions from
`/strategize` (business/UX/technical direction), appended to over time and
read by `/design`'s `analyze.md` step for every feature. `requirements/`
holds design-phase output; `implementations/` holds implement-phase output,
growing per story as `/implement` or `/implement-batch` runs.
`documentation/` holds technical documentation generated by `/pr-create` —
a per-feature `technical-doc.md`, plus cumulative `CHANGELOG.md`,
`api-reference.md`, and `architecture-overview.md` updated across
features.

## Encoding scheme

- `knowledge/config.json` stores the product prefix and counters:
  ```json
  {
    "prefix": "LFC",
    "nextFeatureNumber": 2,
    "nextStoryNumber": { "LFC-001-user-auth": 3 }
  }
  ```
- Created automatically the first time `/design` runs in a project with no
  `config.json` — it will ask for the product name once and derive the
  prefix.
- Feature folders: `<PREFIX>-<NNN>-<slug>` (e.g. `LFC-001-user-auth`).
- Stories: `<PREFIX>-STORY-<NNN>` (e.g. `LFC-STORY-001`), one file per story
  inside the feature's `stories/` dir.
- Requirements live as plain numbered items inside a single `requirements.md`
  per feature (no per-requirement file or code).

## Commands

| Command | Phase | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/strategize` | strategy | Standalone, used on a new project and ongoing. Conversation with the `strategist` agent on business/UX/technical direction, recorded to `knowledge/strategy.md` |
| `/design` | design | Orchestrates `gather` → `analyze` → `draft` → `review` to produce a finalized-when-you-stop set of docs for one feature |
| `/implement` | implement | Implements a single story: `plan` → `execute` → `test` → `commit` |
| `/implement-batch` | implement | Runs `/implement` for every story in a feature folder, sequentially, stopping at the first failure |
| `/pr-create` | git | Manually triggered. Generates technical documentation via `docs-writer`, pushes the feature branch, and opens a PR with a description generated from `implementation-summary.md` |
| `/pr-review` | git | Manually triggered. Spawns the `reviewer` agent with no conversation context to review a PR diff and post comments |

There is no `/finalize` command — whatever is on disk in `knowledge/` when you
stop talking during `/design` is what `/implement` will read.

## Agents

| Agent | Role |
|---|---|
| `strategist` | Helps decide/revise project-wide business, UX, and technical direction. Operates above the per-feature `/design` workflow |
| `orchestrator` | Breaks a story into the agent contributions it needs and sequences them; coordinates plan/execute/test/commit |
| `frontend` | Implements UI/client-side code |
| `backend` | Implements API/server/database code |
| `infrastructure` | Implements Docker, CI/CD, and other infra config |
| `qa` | Writes and runs unit, feature, and end-to-end (Playwright) tests; produces test results |
| `reviewer` | Fresh-context PR reviewer. Receives only the PR diff and `rules/`, no prior conversation. Posts inline comments and a verdict |
| `docs-writer` | Generates technical documentation (per-feature deep dive, changelog, API reference, architecture overview) during `/pr-create` |

A single story is not 1:1 with a single agent — most stories need
contributions from multiple agents (e.g. backend builds an endpoint, frontend
consumes it), sequenced by `orchestrator`.

## Rules

Every agent that writes or tests code must consult, in order:

1. `rules/coding-style.md`
2. `rules/security.md`
3. `rules/testing.md` (specifically the `qa` agent during `test.md`)

## Git conventions

- One branch per feature: `feature/<feature-folder>` (e.g.
  `feature/LFC-001-user-auth`), created on first `/implement` or
  `/implement-batch` run for that feature if it doesn't exist.
- One commit per story, made only after that story's tests pass. The
  `commit.md` step always shows the proposed commit message and the test
  results, and asks for explicit confirmation before committing — it never
  commits silently.
- Commit message format: `<STORY-CODE>: <short summary>` (e.g.
  `LFC-STORY-001: add login form`).
- No automatic PR creation, no automatic merging, ever. `/pr-create` and
  `/pr-review` are separate manual commands, and merging is always a human
  decision.

## On test failure

If `test.md` fails during `/implement` or `/implement-batch`, execution stops
and the failure is surfaced for a decision (fix, skip, or abort) — it is never
auto-retried silently.

<!-- AGENT-FRAMEWORK:END -->
